name: Potato, Leek and Cod Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Finely sliced leek rounds, white parts only (4 large leeks)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and thinly slice the waxy potatoes (5 medium, see note).
2. Soak salt cod overnight, changing water at least once.
3. The next day, drain the salt cod and place it with milk in a pan. Cook over medium heat for 8 minutes.
4. Remove the salt from the pan. Pour the milk through a strainer and set aside.
5. Flake and discard any skin and bones from the cod. Set the cod aside.
6. Melt butter in a clean saucepan over low heat. Add the leeks and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft (about 5 minutes).
7. Stir in the flour. Stir in the reserved milk, potatoes, and pepper. Increase the heat to medium. Cover and cook for 15 minutes.
8. Add the cod and remove from heat. This soup may be made up to a day in advance to this point. Cool, cover, and refrigerate.
9. Place sour cream in a small bowl. Reheat the soup over medium heat until piping hot.
10. Thinly mix the sour cream with half of the hot soup, then pour the mixture into the soup pot. Do not reheat the soup or add more sour cream as it will curdle.
11. Pour the soup into a serving dish, sprinkle with fresh dill, and serve.
Note: Other white fish, such as fresh cod, halibut, or flounder, can be used instead of salt cod.
Note: Use waxy, less starchy potatoes like new potatoes, low Finn, Kennebecks, red rose, or white rose to avoid a mealy texture.
